description: US-02 Issue a Customer Card (Digital) — CardNumber value object, the single issueCard path, and the digital card view. Exactly one valid card per customer; no printing (FD uses a separate system).

userStories.title: CardNumber value object
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a value object for card numbers, so 50k3 is never handled as an ad-hoc string. Source: tasks/prd-us-02-issue-customer-card.md §US-02.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/card/cardNumber.ts exports format(customerNumber, index), parse(text) and next(cardNumber)
format(50, 3) returns '50k3'; parse('50k3') returns {customerNumber: 50, index: 3}; next({50,3}) returns {50,4}
parse returns a typed InvalidCardNumber error for: '', '50', 'k3', '50k0', '50k', '050k3', '50k3x' and negative numbers
Input parsing accepts an uppercase K; output is always lowercase — documented in a comment
Index starts at 1, never 0
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: issueCard use case — the single card-issuing path
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one use case that issues the next card and invalidates the previous one, reused by first issue, loss and stale-count reissue. Source: tasks/prd-us-02-issue-customer-card.md §US-02.2
acceptanceCriteria: issueCard(deps, {customerId, reason}) creates a card with index = max existing index + 1, or 1 if none
Validity is derived from being the highest index — no separate mutable valid flag exists
Rejects with a typed error if the customer is archived
Writes an audit entry with reason FIRST_ISSUE | LOST | STALE_COUNTS | OTHER
Tested with fakes: first issue, second issue, archived customer, issue after a gap in indices (must take max+1)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Card repository with currentCard query
userStories.description: As a developer, I need card persistence that guarantees exactly one valid card per customer. Source: tasks/prd-us-02-issue-customer-card.md §US-02.3
acceptanceCriteria: Card model gains a reason field; @@unique([customerId, index]) is confirmed present
PrismaCardRepository exposes currentCard(customerId) returning the highest index, and issue(customerId, index, reason)
Integration test proves two different customers may both hold card number 50k1
Integration test proves a concurrent double-issue cannot create two cards with the same index — the unique constraint surfaces as a typed error
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Card view at /kunden/[id]/karte
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to see all card information on screen, so I can transcribe it onto the physical card or feed it into the printing system. Source: tasks/prd-us-02-issue-customer-card.md §US-02.4
acceptanceCriteria: Route /kunden/[id]/karte renders card number, first name, last name, group as a German coloured label, grown-up count and children count
Counts are read live from the derived composition, never from a stored column
The view states in German that this is the current card and which numbers it replaced
Layout is card-shaped and legible across a desk; no print stylesheet and no PDF is produced
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: card is issued on registration and displayed
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving registration produces a displayable k1 card. Source: tasks/prd-us-02-issue-customer-card.md §US-02.5
acceptanceCriteria: Spec registers a customer, lands on the card view and asserts the card number matches ^[0-9]+k1$
Spec asserts name, group and counts on the card match the registration input
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
